description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular photovoltaic panels mounted on a large, flat base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, tilted configuration, with two prominent panels elevated at an angle and two others lying flatter on the base. The base itself is textured with a grid-like pattern and includes several blocky support structures, likely serving as mounts or housings for electrical components. The panels appear to be made of dark, glass-like material, while the base and supports are likely constructed from metal or reinforced composite. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or open plazas.
description: A ground-mounted solar array with four rectangular photovoltaic panels on a large, textured base and several blocky support structures. The panels are arranged in a staggered, tilted configuration for optimal sunlight capture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the staggered arrangement of the solar panels, with two panels elevated and two lying flatter on the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angles of the two elevated panels and the blocky support structures beneath them.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the panels and the full extent of the base, with support structures visible at the corners.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tilt of the panels and the spatial relationship between the supports and the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 30-40 degrees, facing upward and slightly offset from the base; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with blocky support structures, likely made of metal or reinforced composite.
attribute_array_configuration: Cluster of four panels in a staggered, non-linear arrangement, with two panels elevated and two flatter on the base.
